steps.title: Spot the defining claim
steps.content: The passage's core concept is that values are a “luxury good”—higher income grants “liberty to assign weight to values when voting.” The key signal: economic security enables people to prioritize moral values when voting, even when those values conflict with their financial self-interest.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D states that high-income people are “enabled to take certain considerations into account when voting“ — their economic security allows them to prioritize values.

The other three either invent a false alignment between values and interests (B), misidentify the relationship between income and value-conflict (A), or discuss political strategy beyond the passage's scope (C).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
